topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sound of his voice, melodic and fluid, drifting from the corner of the room, hit me like a physical blow. I watched him, my jaw tightening, as he effortlessly navigated a complex sentence in my mother tongue. It wasn't just casual conversation; it was deliberate, practiced. A hot flush crept up my neck, warming my cheeks. I kept my hands folded tightly in my lap, focusing intently on the pattern of the rug beneath my feet, trying to appear utterly absorbed in something mundane. Every syllable he uttered felt like a tiny, sharp pebble lodged beneath my skin. When he finally turned to me, his eyes crinkled at the corners, a warm, knowing smile playing on his lips. "Just practicing," he murmured, his accent softening the unfamiliar sounds. The casualness of it only made the tension coil tighter in my chest.

[story 2]

I caught him late one evening, headphones on, whispering something rapid and intricate to a friend on a video call. The words were fast, almost musical, and I couldn't quite decipher the nuances, but the rhythm was undeniably familiar. A knot formed low in my stomach, a churning sensation that made my breath catch. I stood by the doorway, pretending to check my phone, but my gaze was locked on the exchange. When the call ended, he turned, catching my stare. There was a flicker of something—a quick, almost apologetic glance—before he smoothly transitioned into English. "Just reviewing some phrases," he said, his tone light. But I could see the slight hesitation in his eyes, a momentary vulnerability that only amplified the tightness gripping my throat. I walked into the kitchen, needing distance, my movements stiff and overly deliberate.

[story 3]

It happened over dinner. He was recounting a story from his travels, and suddenly, he switched gears. He wasn't just retelling the events; he was weaving in archaic vocabulary, the cadence shifting entirely. I felt a distinct clenching in my fists under the table, a need to physically ground myself. My attention fractured; I started fiddling incessantly with my silverware, the clink of the fork against the plate sounding deafening in the sudden silence. When he paused, waiting for a reaction, I forced a small, brittle laugh. "That was... elaborate," I managed, my voice sounding strained. He looked surprised, a slight furrow in his brow, but he didn't press. Instead, he leaned in, his expression softening, and spoke a few words in the language I knew intimately. The intimacy of the sound, directed solely at me, made my entire body feel suddenly too exposed, too keenly aware of the boundary he was testing.
